formality.description: Bengali has a three-tier pronoun system: আপনি (apni, formal/respectful), তুমি (tumi, familiar), and তুই (tui, intimate/condescending). Software UI uses আপনি. Bengali also distinguishes between শুদ্ধ ভাষা (shuddho bhasha, standard/literary) and চলিত ভাষা (cholito bhasha, colloquial). Modern software uses cholito bhasha.

gender.inclusiveGuidance: Bengali pronouns are gender-neutral. Third-person pronouns (সে/তিনি) do not distinguish gender.

professional.label: চলিত (Professional)
professional.description: Standard for software UI. Colloquial standard (চলিত ভাষা) with আপনি address.
professional.prompt: Professional Bengali (চলিত ভাষা/cholito bhasha). Use আপনি (apni) for respectful second-person address. Modern colloquial standard — not literary/sadhu bhasha. Clear, accessible vocabulary appropriate for software interfaces.

formal-shuddho.label: শুদ্ধ (Formal/Literary)
formal-shuddho.description: For government, academic, or literary contexts using standard literary Bengali.
formal-shuddho.prompt: Formal literary Bengali (শুদ্ধ ভাষা/shuddho bhasha). Elevated register with literary verb forms. For government, academic, and formal institutional contexts.
